channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump-backed plan to avert shutdown heads for House vote
item.description: House lawmakers are poised to vote on a bill supported by President Donald Trump to avert a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former state Sen. López Franzen announces U.S. Senate run in Minnesota
item.description: Melisa López Franzen, a former Democratic leader in the Legislature, said she will run for Minnesota’s open U.S. Senate seat next year. She most recently worked at the University of Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Scoop: Trump presses GOP rebels ahead of critical government shutdown vote
item.description: House Republicans are getting help from the Trump White House to get their bill to avert a partial government shutdown over the line.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EPA froze 'green bank' funds worth billions, climate group suit says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A nonprofit that was awarded nearly $7 billion by the Biden administration to finance clean energy and climate-friendly projects has sued President Donald Trump's Environmental Protection Agency, accusing it of improperly freezing a legally awarded grant.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY and MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Americans have clear opinions on Trump's performance in his first 50 days
item.description: As President Donald Trump marks 50 days into his second tour of duty in the White House, the most recent polls suggest that Americans are divided on the job he's doing
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harvard freezes hiring amid financial 'uncertainties' driven by Trump policies
item.description: Harvard University says it is freezing hiring for faculty and staff amid financial “uncertainties” driven by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Protestors dispute Vance’s description of clash on street over Ukraine
item.description: In a post on X, Vance said he was confronted by pro-Ukraine protestors while he was with his 3-year-old daughter on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to sign disaster relief order putting states, localities in the driver's seat of catastrophe response
item.description: President Donald Trump is expected to sign an executive order Monday that will emphasize the role localities play in preparing for and responding to disasters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: OPM inspector general investigating DOGE emails, access to agency data
item.description: The inspector general for the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) told lawmakers he is investigating the use of a new data system at the agency that has been part of a broader effort by Elon Musk and his allies to email federal workers.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US ‘launched a drug war, not a trade war’ with Mexico, Canada: Hassett
item.description: Hassett later added that the administration hopes to have solved the drug war “by the end of the month.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: China learned from Trump's first trade war and changed its tactics when tariffs came again
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The leaders of both Canada and Mexico got on the phone with President Donald Trump this past week to seek solutions after he slapped tariffs on their countries, but China’s president appears unlikely to make a similar call soon.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Republicans unveil bill to avoid shutdown and they're daring Democrats to oppose it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Republicans unveiled a spending bill Saturday that would keep federal agencies funded through Sept. 30, pushing ahead with a go-it-alone strategy that seems certain to spark a major confrontation with Democrats over the contours of government spending.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump shifts tone on Musk as tensions rise with Cabinet
item.description: President Trump is signaling a softer approach to the mass firings of federal government workers led by Elon Musk as cracks emerge between the tech billionaire and Cabinet secretaries running the agencies he’s gutting.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: House GOP seeks spending cuts, defense increases to avert shutdown
item.description: The bill's rollout kicks off a crucial stretch for GOP leadership to lock down support ahead of Friday's shutdown deadline with a Trump-backed strategy that has drawn the ire of Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Al Green censure highlights increased appetite for retribution in House
item.description: A formal House reprimand didn't contain the fallout from Rep. Al Green (D-Texas) interrupting President Trump’s speech to Congress, showing how petty fights and demands for retribution have become an increasingly prominent part of business on the House floor.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump will lead task force preparing for 2026 World Cup
item.description: President Donald Trump has created a task force to prepare for the 2026 World Cup, which will bring the globe's premier soccer tournament to North America at a time when his on-again, off-again tariffs have ratcheted up tensions across the continent.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Unions ask court to stop DOGE from accessing Social Security data of millions of Americans
item.description: A group of labor unions are asking a federal court for an emergency order to stop Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency from accessing the sensitive Social Security data of millions of Americans. The motion for emergency relief was filed late Friday in federal court in Maryland.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Ras Baraka has highest favorability among Democratic candidates for NJ governor: poll
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – New polling from Fairleigh Dickinson University gave us some early insight on who voters are looking into in the race for New Jersey governor. Democratic voters weighed in on the candidates' favorability.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Renewed debate sparks discussion on 'My Safe Florida Home' program
item.description: Governor Ron DeSantis wants more Floridians to benefit from the "My Safe Florida Home" program, which provides storm-hardening home improvements for homeowners.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Politics Friday: Moves in Washington hover over state budget outlook
item.description: MPR News senior politics editor Brian Bakst talked to Sen. Mark Johnson about the state’s budget situation. Plus, a conversation with the dean of Minnesota’s congressional delegation, U.S. Rep. Betty McCollum.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Tribal nations are concerned that Trump's cuts have the potential to violate trust responsibilities
item.description: Tribal leaders across the United States are scrambling to respond to a directive from President Donald Trump and Elon Musk to close more than a quarter of Bureau of Indian Affairs offices, which provide vital services to Indigenous communities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump changes course and delays some tariffs on Mexico and Canada
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday postponed 25 percent tariffs on many imports from Mexico and some imports from Canada for a month amid widespread fears of the economic fallout from a broader trade war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Hillsborough commission votes against asking for state DOGE audit
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — The newly formed Florida State Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) task force is set to use technology and AI to help look for government waste. It will audit colleges and universities, state agencies, along with local governments.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada Democrats urge Lombardo to hire fired federal workers
item.description: Democratic leaders in the Nevada Assembly are urging Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo to hire federal workers laid off as part of recent firings from President Donald Trump and Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What Trump’s aid freeze means for Ukraine war
item.description: President Donald Trump’s freeze on U.S. aid going to Ukraine threatens to create severe problems for Kyiv in its war against Russia, leaving the country more vulnerable to aerial attacks on population centers and key infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada 'a perfect storm for homicides' in domestic violence cases, lawmakers told
item.description: FBI statistics show Nevada is "a perfect storm for homicides" and the state ranks third for men murdering women, the leader of SafeNest said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 46 percent say Trump changing economy for the worse: Survey
item.description: Recent polling has indicated that U.S. adults’ confidence in Trump’s ability to manage the country’s economy — the top issue in the 2024 presidential election — has been slipping.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump trade war intensifies as tariffs go into place
item.description: Americans awoke on Tuesday morning to an intensifying trade war, as President Donald Trump’s newest tariffs on Canadian, Mexican and Chinese imports were imposed and those countries quickly moved to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5
